modules.name: core
modules.summary: Global builtins available in every Ion program without `use`.

members.name: len
members.doc: Length of list, string, dict, or bytes

members.name: assert_eq
members.doc: Error if values are not equal

members.name: set
members.doc: Create a set from a list (deduplicates elements)

members.name: cell
members.doc: Create a mutable reference cell for shared closure state

members.name: sleep
members.doc: Sleep for given milliseconds (requires concurrency feature)

members.name: timeout
members.doc: Run function with time limit, returns Option (Some or None on timeout)

modules.name: types
modules.summary: Built-in value types and the methods callable on them.

methods.name: split
methods.doc: Split by byte value or bytes separator

methods.name: replace
methods.doc: Replace byte value or bytes sequence

methods.name: set
methods.doc: Replace byte at index, returning new bytes

methods.name: read_u64_le
methods.doc: Read unsigned 64-bit little-endian integer if it fits in int

methods.name: read_u64_be
methods.doc: Read unsigned 64-bit big-endian integer if it fits in int

methods.name: chunk
methods.doc: Split into chunks of size n

methods.name: reduce
methods.doc: Reduce list with fn (no initial value)

methods.name: map
methods.doc: Apply fn(key, value) to each entry and keep keys

methods.name: filter
methods.doc: Keep entries where fn(key, value) is truthy

methods.name: len
methods.doc: Number of values in the range

methods.name: contains
methods.doc: True if the value is in range

methods.name: to_list
methods.doc: Materialize the range as a list

methods.name: update
methods.doc: Apply fn to the current value, store it, and return it

methods.name: map
methods.doc: Apply fn to the inner value if Some

methods.name: unwrap_or_else
methods.doc: Unwrap or call fn if None

methods.name: map
methods.doc: Apply fn to the Ok value

methods.name: unwrap_or_else
methods.doc: Unwrap or call fn with the error

members.name: Task
members.doc: Async task handle returned by `spawn`. Requires the `concurrency` feature.

methods.name: await_timeout
methods.doc: Wait up to ms and return Option

members.name: Channel
members.doc: Bounded async channel created by `channel(n)`. Requires the `concurrency` feature.

members.name: AsyncTask
members.doc: Native async-runtime task handle returned by `spawn`.

members.name: AsyncChannelSender
members.doc: Native async-runtime channel sender returned by `channel(n)`.

methods.name: send
methods.doc: Send, parking if the channel is full

members.name: AsyncChannelReceiver
members.doc: Native async-runtime channel receiver returned by `channel(n)`.

methods.name: recv
methods.doc: Receive, parking until a value arrives or the channel closes

members.name: msgpack_encode
members.doc: Encode value as MessagePack bytes. Requires the `msgpack` cargo feature.

members.name: msgpack_decode
members.doc: Decode MessagePack bytes into a value. Requires the `msgpack` cargo feature.

modules.name: bytes
modules.summary: Byte construction, encoding, concatenation, and endian packing.

members.name: repeat
members.doc: Create bytes filled with a repeated byte.

members.name: from_list
members.doc: Create bytes from byte values in the range 0..=255.

members.name: from_str
members.doc: Encode a string as UTF-8 bytes.

members.name: join
members.doc: Join bytes values with an optional bytes separator.

members.name: u64_le
members.doc: Pack unsigned 64-bit little-endian integer that fits in Ion int.

members.name: u64_be
members.doc: Pack unsigned 64-bit big-endian integer that fits in Ion int.

modules.name: rand
modules.summary: Random values, ranges, and collection sampling.

members.name: int
members.doc: Random integer. Bounded forms use half-open ranges.

members.name: float
members.doc: Random float in a half-open range.

members.name: bool
members.doc: Random boolean, optionally true with probability 0.0..=1.0.

members.name: choice
members.doc: Random element, or None for an empty input.

members.name: to_string
members.doc: Convert a value to a string.

members.name: join
members.doc: Join list elements into string with optional separator.

modules.name: log
modules.summary: Leveled logging — `log::trace` / `debug` / `info` / `warn` / `error`. Compile-time stripped above the cap.

members.name: trace
members.doc: Emit a TRACE record. Stripped at compile time when level > log_max_level_*.

members.name: debug
members.doc: Emit a DEBUG record. Stripped at compile time when level > log_max_level_*.

members.name: info
members.doc: Emit an INFO record. Stripped at compile time when level > log_max_level_*.

members.name: warn
members.doc: Emit a WARN record. Stripped at compile time when level > log_max_level_*.

members.name: level
members.doc: Return the current runtime threshold as a string.

members.name: enabled
members.doc: Check whether the handler would emit at the given level.

modules.name: semver
modules.summary: Semantic version parsing, comparison, and constraint matching.

members.name: parse
members.doc: Parse a version string into `#{major, minor, patch, pre, build}`. Errors on invalid input.

members.name: is_valid
members.doc: True if the string parses as a valid semantic version.

members.name: format
members.doc: Render a version (string or dict) back to its canonical string form.

members.name: eq
members.doc: True if `a` and `b` are the same version (including pre-release).

members.name: satisfies
members.doc: True if `version` matches the requirement string (e.g. `^1.0`, `~1.2`, `>=1.0, <2.0`).

members.name: bump_major
members.doc: Increment major; zero minor and patch; clear pre-release and build.

members.name: bump_minor
members.doc: Increment minor; zero patch; clear pre-release and build.

members.name: bump_patch
members.doc: Increment patch (or strip pre-release if present); clear build.

modules.name: os
modules.summary: OS / arch detection, environment variables, and process info.

members.name: dll_extension
members.doc: Dynamic-library extension without the leading dot ("so", "dylib", "dll").

members.name: exe_extension
members.doc: Executable extension without the leading dot ("" on Unix, "exe" on Windows).

members.name: env_var
members.doc: Read an env var. Errors if missing; the optional 2nd arg is returned instead.

members.name: has_env_var
members.doc: True if the named env var is set in the current process.

members.name: env_vars
members.doc: Snapshot of all env vars as a `dict<string, string>`.

members.name: sep
members.doc: Platform path separator — `/` on Unix, `\` on Windows.

members.name: join
members.doc: Variadic path join using the platform separator.

members.name: parent
members.doc: Directory containing `p`. Empty string if `p` has no parent.

members.name: stem
members.doc: Basename of `p` with the extension stripped.

members.name: extension
members.doc: Extension of `p` without the leading dot. Empty string if none.

members.name: is_absolute
members.doc: True if `p` is absolute on the current platform.

members.name: is_relative
members.doc: True if `p` is relative on the current platform.

members.name: normalize
members.doc: Lexically normalise `p` — collapse `.` and `..` without consulting the filesystem.

modules.name: fs
modules.summary: Filesystem I/O. Sync (`std::fs`) or async (`tokio::fs`) impl picked by the build's runtime feature.

members.name: read
members.doc: Read the file at `path` as UTF-8 text.

members.name: read_bytes
members.doc: Read the file at `path` as raw bytes.

members.name: write
members.doc: Write `contents` (string or bytes) to `path`, replacing the existing file.

members.name: append
members.doc: Append `contents` (string or bytes) to `path`. Creates the file if missing.

members.name: append_random
members.doc: Append count random bytes to `path`; returns bytes appended. Creates the file if missing.

members.name: pad_random
members.doc: Grow `path` to target_size with random bytes; returns bytes appended.

members.name: is_file
members.doc: True if `path` is an existing regular file.

members.name: is_dir
members.doc: True if `path` is an existing directory.

members.name: list_dir
members.doc: Names of the entries directly inside the directory at `path` (non-recursive).

members.name: create_dir
members.doc: Create a single directory. Errors if any parent is missing.

members.name: create_dir_all
members.doc: Create the directory and any missing parents.

members.name: remove_dir
members.doc: Delete the directory at `path`. Errors if not empty.

members.name: remove_dir_all
members.doc: Recursively delete the directory at `path` and all its contents.

members.name: canonicalize
members.doc: Resolve symlinks and normalise `path` against the filesystem.
